title: Glasses
description: Adds wearable glasses with a prescription system, assigned players need their glasses to see clearly, while others get blurred vision from wearing the wrong prescription. Features a custom "Poor Vision" fog effect, Trinkets support.
body:
## What is Glasses?

Glasses is a Fabric mod that adds wearable glasses to Minecraft. There are two styles to choose from, both craftable and equippable in the helmet slot.

The twist: glasses come with a prescription system. Server ops can assign glasses to specific players using the `/glasses assign` command — those players *need* their glasses to see properly. Take them off and you'll get hit with a "Poor Vision" effect that fills your screen with thick fog and a dark vignette. 

On the flip side, if you're *not* assigned and you put on someone else's glasses, you'll get an even worse version of the effect — because it's the wrong prescription.

## Features

- **Two glasses styles** that render as 3D models on your head (Blockbench models, not flat textures)
- **Prescription system** — ops can assign/remove glasses for players, and it persists across sessions
- **Custom "Poor Vision" effect** — not just vanilla blindness, it uses fog and a vignette overlay so you can still kind of see
- **Y-offset adjustment** — use `/glasses setyoffset` to move the glasses up or down on your head so they line up with your skin's eyes
- **Trinkets support** — if you have Trinkets installed, glasses can go in a trinket slot instead of the helmet slot

## Commands

All op-only except `setyoffset`:

- `/glasses assign <player>` — prescribe glasses to a player
- `/glasses remove <player>` — remove a player's prescription
- `/glasses info <player>` — check a player's assignment status
- `/glasses setyoffset <pixels>` — adjust vertical position of your glasses (-4 to 4)

## Requirements

- Minecraft 1.20.1
- Fabric Loader 0.18.4+
- Fabric API

Trinkets is optional but supported.
